traits.name: Rare
traits.text: This rarity indicates that a rules element is very difficult to find in the game world. A rare feat, spell, item or the like is available to players only if the GM decides to include it in the game, typically through discovery during play. Creatures with this trait are rare. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creatures is increased by 5.

traits.name: Aberration
traits.text: Aberrations are creatures from beyond the planes or corruptions of the natural order.

sources.name: Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star

link.name: Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star pg. 56
link.alt: Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star pg. 56

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Razing
traits.text: Razing weapons are particularly good at damaging objects, structures, and vehicles. Whenever you deal damage to an object (including shields and animated objects), structure, or vehicle with a razing weapon, the object takes an amount of additional damage equal to double the number of weapon damage dice.

traits.name: Reach
traits.text: This weapon can be used to attack enemies up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent enemies. For creatures with reach, the weapon increases their reach by 5 feet.

traits.name: Shove
traits.text: You can use this weapon to Shove with the Athletics skill even if you don’t have a free hand. This uses the weapon’s reach (if different from your own) and adds the weapon’s item bonus to attack rolls as an item bonus to the Athletics check. If you critically fail a check to Shove using the weapon, you can drop the weapon to take the effects of a failure instead of a critical failure.

traits.name: Versatile
traits.text: A versatile weapon can be used to deal a different type of damage than its listed type. This trait indicates the alternate damage type. For instance, a piercing weapon with versatile S can deal piercing or slashing damage. You choose the damage type each time you attack.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

ability.name: False Pride

traits.name: Arcane
traits.text:
This magic comes from the arcane tradition, which is built on logic and rationality. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to arcane magic.

traits.name: Illusion
traits.text: Effects and magic items with this trait involve false sensory stimuli.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

traits.name: Visual
traits.text: A visual effect can affect only creatures that can see it. This applies only to visible parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.

ability.success: The creature becomes overconfident and careless as they believe themselves to be more  intimidating than they actually are. They're off-guard against the next attack the pridespawn sentinel attempts against them this round.
ability.failure: As success, but the creature's false pride causes them to become slowed 1 for 1 round as they spend one of their actions posturing and bragging about their superiority. They're off-guard as long as they remain slowed.
ability.text: The pridespawn sentinel stumbles back and appears frightened and intimidated by a creature within 30 feet that it can see. The targeted creature must attempt a DC 32 Will save; regardless of the result, that creature is temporarily immune to False Pride for 24 hours.

ability.name: Sinful Bite

traits.name: Arcane
traits.text:
This magic comes from the arcane tradition, which is built on logic and rationality. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to arcane magic.

traits.name: Emotion
traits.text: This effect alters a creature’s emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

ability.critical_failure: The creature is sickened 2 and also clumsy 1 and enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
ability.text: A creature hit by the jaws of a pridespawn sentinel must attempt a DC 29 Will save as it's assailed by arrogant thoughts.

ability.name: Sneak Attack
ability.text: A pridespawn sentinel deals 1d6 extra precision damage to creatures who are off-guard.

universal_monster_ability.name: Sneak Attack
universal_monster_ability.text: When the monster Strikes a creature that has the flat-footed condition with an agile or finesse melee weapon, an agile or finesse unarmed attack, or a ranged weapon attack, it also deals the listed precision damage. For a ranged attack with a thrown weapon, that weapon must also be an agile or finesse weapon.
